title: forge-kaif
description: Machine-readable API manifest for AI agent tool discovery. Describes the full public API surface of the forge-kaif package — including module classes, functions, type signatures, docstrings, error types, and usage examples.

forge.config.description: Typed, validated, layered configuration from env vars, .env, and TOML files via pydantic-settings. All modules declare their config requirements as Pydantic models; validation happens at startup.

error_types.name: ConfigurationError

classes.name: ForgeConfig
classes.description: Root configuration model. Auto-populated from environment variables, .env files, and forge.config.toml. Subclasses pydantic-settings BaseSettings.

classes.name: ConfigModule
classes.signature: ConfigModule(config_path: Path | str | None = None, env_file: Path | str | None = None) -> ConfigModule
classes.description: ForgeModule that provides validated application configuration. Loads from env vars, .env files, and forge.config.toml. Must be registered first in the runtime.

classes.name: AIConfig
classes.signature: AIConfig(default_model: str = 'gpt-4o', openai_api_key: SecretStr | None = None, anthropic_api_key: SecretStr | None = None, timeout: float = 30.0, max_tokens: int = 4096, fallback_models: list[str] = [], structured_output_retries: int = 3) -> AIConfig
classes.description: Configuration for the forge AI module.

classes.name: LogConfig
classes.signature: LogConfig(level: str = 'INFO', format: str = 'dev', levels: dict[str, str] = {}) -> LogConfig
classes.description: Configuration for the forge logging module.

classes.name: RetryConfig
classes.signature: RetryConfig(default_attempts: int = 3, default_backoff: str = 'exponential', default_base_delay: float = 1.0, default_max_delay: float = 60.0, default_jitter: bool = True) -> RetryConfig
classes.description: Configuration for the forge retry module.

classes.name: CacheConfig
classes.signature: CacheConfig(backend: str = 'memory', default_ttl: int = 300, memory_max_size: int = 1000, redis_url: SecretStr | None = None) -> CacheConfig
classes.description: Configuration for the forge cache module.

classes.name: HealthConfig
classes.description: Configuration for the forge health module.

classes.name: CircuitBreakerConfig
classes.description: Configuration for the forge circuit breaker.

functions.name: load_dotenv
functions.description: Parse a .env file into a dict. Handles quoted values, comments, and multi-line values.

functions.name: load_toml
functions.description: Load a TOML config file. Returns empty dict if file not found.

functions.name: merge_config
functions.description: Deep-merge config layers. Later layers win on conflict. Nested dicts are merged recursively.

functions.name: deep_merge
functions.signature: deep_merge(base: dict[str, Any], override: dict[str, Any]) -> None
functions.description: In-place deep merge of override into base dict.

functions.name: mask_value
functions.description: Mask a sensitive string showing only the last N characters.

functions.name: str_from_secret
functions.description: Safely extract the string value from a SecretStr. Returns None if secret is None.

functions.name: field_is_sensitive
functions.description: Returns True if the field name suggests it contains sensitive data. Matches against known patterns like 'key', 'token', 'password', 'secret'.

functions.name: is_secret
functions.description: Returns True if the value is a SecretStr or SecretBytes.

forge.log.description: Structured JSON logging with automatic trace context propagation via contextvars. Supports development (colorized) and production (JSON) output formats.

classes.name: LogModule
classes.description: ForgeModule that provides structured logging. Automatically registers JSON and dev formatters based on config. Must be initialized after ConfigModule.

classes.name: LoggerProxy
classes.description: Proxy around stdlib Logger that enforces structured logging (no %-formatting). All extra kwargs become structured fields.

classes.name: LogContext
classes.description: Context manager that binds key-value pairs to all log entries within the context block.

classes.name: LogContextFilter
classes.description: Logging filter that injects contextvars into LogRecord.

classes.name: JSONFormatter
classes.description: Production log formatter. Outputs one JSON object per line, designed for log aggregators (Datadog, CloudWatch, Loki).

classes.name: DevFormatter
classes.description: Development log formatter. Human-readable, colorized, compact output with ANSI codes. NOT for production use.

functions.name: get
functions.description: Get a logger instance for the given module name. Delegates to the active runtime's LogModule if initialized, otherwise returns a default LoggerProxy.

functions.name: context
functions.description: Context manager for binding key-value pairs to all log entries within the context block. Pairs with log.context() for request-scoped data.
functions.example:
with context(request_id='abc-123', user_id=42):
    log.info('Processing')  # includes request_id and user_id

forge.retry.description: Retry logic with exponential backoff, full jitter, and circuit breaker. Zero overhead on the success path. Automatically logs retry attempts when LogModule is registered.

error_types.name: RetryError
error_types.description: Raised when retries are exhausted. Includes attempt count and last exception.

error_types.name: NonRetryableError
error_types.description: Raised when a non-retryable exception is encountered during a retry block.

error_types.name: CircuitBreakerOpenError
error_types.description: Raised when a request is blocked because the circuit breaker is OPEN. Format includes failure count, threshold, and recovery time.

classes.name: CircuitBreaker
classes.signature: CircuitBreaker(failure_threshold: int = 5, recovery_time: float = 60.0, name: str = 'unnamed') -> CircuitBreaker
classes.description: Circuit breaker that prevents retries after a configurable failure threshold. State machine: CLOSED -> OPEN -> HALF_OPEN -> CLOSED.

classes.name: RetryModule
classes.description: ForgeModule that provides retry configuration and integrates with the logging module for automatic retry attempt logging.

classes.name: CircuitBreakerState
classes.description: Enum of circuit breaker states: CLOSED (normal), OPEN (blocking), HALF_OPEN (probing).

functions.name: retry
functions.signature: retry(attempts: int = 3, backoff: str | BackoffStrategy = 'exponential', exceptions: tuple[type[Exception], ...] | None = None, circuit_breaker: CircuitBreaker | None = None, on_retry: Callable[[int, Exception], None] | None = None) -> Callable[[F], F]
functions.description: Decorator for async functions that retries on failure with configurable backoff. Supports exponential, linear, and constant backoff strategies. Only retries on specified exception types. Non-retryable exceptions pass through immediately.
functions.example:
@retry(attempts=3, backoff='exponential', exceptions=(ConnectionError, TimeoutError))
async def fetch_data(): ...

functions.name: exponential
functions.signature: exponential(base: float = 1.0, maximum: float = 60.0, jitter: bool = True, multiplier: float = 2.0) -> ExponentialBackoff
functions.description: Create an exponential backoff strategy with full jitter (AWS-recommended).

functions.name: linear
functions.description: Create a linear backoff strategy: delay = base * (attempt + 1).

functions.name: constant
functions.description: Create a constant backoff strategy (fixed delay between retries). Useful for rate limit handling.

functions.name: get_backoff
functions.description: Resolve a backoff strategy by name: 'exponential', 'linear', or 'constant'.

forge.ai.description: Unified AI model interface across OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and Ollama. Supports completions, streaming, structured output enforcement (Pydantic with auto-retry), model fallback chains, token budgeting, and cost estimation.

error_types.name: AIError
error_types.description: Base exception for all AI module errors.

error_types.name: AIProviderError
error_types.description: Raised when the provider API returns an error (auth, billing, bad request).

error_types.name: RateLimitError
error_types.description: Raised when the provider rate-limits the client. Includes retry-after information.

error_types.name: TokenLimitError
error_types.description: Raised when the requested prompt exceeds the model's context window.

error_types.name: ModelNotFoundError
error_types.description: Raised when the requested model is not available or registered with any adapter.

error_types.name: AllModelsFailedError
error_types.description: Raised when all models in the fallback chain have failed.

error_types.name: StructuredOutputError
error_types.description: Raised when structured output fails after max retries. Contains schema_name, attempts, last_response, and last_error.

error_types.name: StreamInterruptedError
error_types.description: Raised when an active stream is cut short or disconnected.

error_types.name: BudgetExceededError
error_types.description: Raised when a request exceeds the configured token budget before execution.

classes.name: Message
classes.description: An immutable chat message. Use factory methods: Message.system(), Message.user(), Message.assistant() for role validation.
classes.example:
msg = Message.user('What is 2+2?')
msg = Message.system('You are helpful.')

classes.name: AIResponse
classes.signature: AIResponse(content: str, model: str, provider: str, input_tokens: int, output_tokens: int, latency_ms: float, parsed: Any | None = None, finish_reason: str = 'stop', request_id: str | None = None) -> AIResponse
classes.description: Immutable response from an AI model completion call. Includes token counts, latency, cost estimate, and optional parsed structured output.

classes.name: CompletionRequest
classes.signature: CompletionRequest(model: str, messages: list[Message], max_tokens: int | None = None, temperature: float | None = None, **extra: Any) -> CompletionRequest
classes.description: Request object for AI completion calls. Holds model, messages, and generation parameters.

classes.name: CompletionResponse
classes.signature: CompletionResponse(content: str, model: str, usage: TokenUsage) -> CompletionResponse
classes.description: Standard completion response with content, model info, and token usage.

classes.name: StreamChunk
classes.signature: StreamChunk(delta: str, model: str, provider: str, finish_reason: str | None = None, input_tokens: int | None = None, output_tokens: int | None = None) -> StreamChunk
classes.description: A single chunk in a streaming AI response. The final chunk has finish_reason and token counts populated.
classes.example:
async for chunk in stream(messages=[Message.user('Hello')]):
    print(chunk.delta, end='')

classes.name: TokenUsage
classes.description: Token usage breakdown for an AI request.

classes.name: AIModule
classes.description: ForgeModule that provides AI model abstraction. Manages adapters, model routing, retry integration, and logging for all AI calls.

classes.name: ModelRouter
classes.description: Routes AI requests to the appropriate provider adapter based on model name. Supports exact match, prefix match, and explicit provider/model notation ('openai/gpt-4o').

classes.name: TokenCounter
classes.description: Token counting utility for estimating usage before making API calls. Uses provider-specific tokenizers when available.

classes.name: BaseAdapter
classes.description: Abstract base class for all AI provider adapters. Subclasses must implement: provider_name, supported_models, complete(), stream(). Must NOT implement retry or logging (handled by AIModule).

classes.name: OpenAIAdapter
classes.signature: OpenAIAdapter(api_key: str, base_url: str | None = None, timeout: float = 30.0, max_retries: int = 0) -> OpenAIAdapter
classes.description: Adapter for OpenAI-compatible APIs (OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Groq, Together, etc.). Requires openai>=1.12 package.

classes.name: AnthropicAdapter
classes.description: Adapter for Anthropic's Claude API. Requires anthropic>=0.20 package. Handles system message extraction and different streaming semantics.

classes.name: GeminiAdapter
classes.description: Adapter for Google Gemini API.

classes.name: OllamaAdapter
classes.description: Adapter for local Ollama models.

classes.name: MockAdapter
classes.signature: MockAdapter(response: str | Callable | None = None, responses: list[str] | None = None, fail_with: Exception | None = None, fail_on_attempt: int | None = None, latency: float = 0.0) -> MockAdapter
classes.description: Test adapter for unit testing without real API calls. Supports fixed/dynamic/sequenced responses and simulated failures.

functions.name: complete
functions.signature: complete(messages: list[Message], model: str | None = None, max_tokens: int | None = None, temperature: float | None = None, output_schema: type[BaseModel] | None = None, fallback_models: list[str] | None = None, max_retries: int | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> CompletionResponse | BaseModel
functions.description: Send a completion request to an AI model. Supports structured output via Pydantic schemas with automatic retry on invalid output, and fallback model chains. Uses the active ForgeRuntime's AIModule.
functions.example:
response = await complete(messages=[Message.user('Hello')], model='gpt-4o')
print(response.content)

result = await complete(messages=[...], output_schema=SentimentResult)
print(result.sentiment)

functions.name: stream
functions.signature: stream(messages: list[Message], model: str | None = None, max_tokens: int | None = None, temperature: float | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> AsyncIterator[StreamChunk]
functions.description: Stream a completion response from an AI model. Yields StreamChunk objects with delta content. Final chunk includes finish_reason and token counts. Uses the active ForgeRuntime's AIModule.
functions.example:
async for chunk in stream(messages=[Message.user('Tell me a story')]):
    print(chunk.delta, end='')

forge.health.description: Kubernetes-compatible health and readiness endpoints with custom check registration. Provides /health (liveness) and /ready (readiness) FastAPI routers.

error_types.name: HealthCheckError
error_types.description: Raised when a health check fails critically.

classes.name: HealthModule
classes.description: ForgeModule that manages health and readiness checks. Automatically collects health_check() from all registered modules. Provides FastAPI router with /health and /ready endpoints.

classes.name: HealthRegistry
classes.description: Registry for custom health check functions. Supports async checks with configurable timeout.

classes.name: HealthResult
classes.signature: HealthResult(status: CheckStatus, message: str | None = None, latency_ms: float | None = None) -> HealthResult
classes.description: Result of a single health check. Status is 'ok', 'degraded', or 'error'. Use factory methods: HealthResult.ok(), HealthResult.degraded(), HealthResult.error().

functions.name: check
functions.signature: check(name: str) -> Callable[[Callable[..., Awaitable[HealthResult]]], Callable[..., Awaitable[HealthResult]]]
functions.description: Decorator for registering a custom async health check function. The decorated function must return a HealthResult.

forge.cache.description: Decorator-based caching with in-memory LRU and Redis backends. Supports configurable TTL, custom cache keys, and manual invalidation.

error_types.name: CacheError
error_types.description: Base exception for all cache errors.

error_types.name: CacheBackendError
error_types.description: Raised when a cache backend operation fails (connection, serialization, etc.).

classes.name: CacheModule
classes.description: ForgeModule that provides caching infrastructure. Initializes the configured cache backend (memory or Redis) and provides default instances.

functions.name: cached
functions.signature: cached(ttl: int = 300, key: str | None = None, backend: CacheBackend | None = None) -> Callable[[F], F]
functions.description: Decorator that caches async function results with configurable TTL. Supports key templates with {param_name} substitution. If no key is provided, auto-generates from function name + args hash.

functions.name: invalidate
functions.description: Invalidate a cached entry by key. Uses the active cache module's delete method.

forge.validation.description: Pydantic-integrated input validation with consistent error responses. Provides a decorator that validates function arguments against Pydantic schemas and raises FastAPI-compatible 422 errors.

classes.name: ValidationModule
classes.description: ForgeModule that provides validation infrastructure.

classes.name: ValidationErrorDetail
classes.signature: ValidationErrorDetail(loc: list[str | int], msg: str, type: str) -> ValidationErrorDetail
classes.description: Details of a single validation error, compatible with FastAPI error response format.

classes.name: ValidationErrorResponse
classes.description: Standard error response for validation failures. Produces consistent 422 responses.

functions.name: validate
functions.description: Decorator that validates function input against a Pydantic schema. Works with both sync and async functions. On validation error, raises HTTPException(422) with structured error details.

functions.name: format_validation_error
functions.description: Convert a Pydantic ValidationError into a standard ValidationErrorResponse with consistent format.

forge.core.description: Core runtime with module registry, DI container, event bus, and lifecycle management. Provides the ForgeModule base class and ForgeRuntime coordinator for module initialization ordering and dependency injection.

error_types.name: ForgeError
error_types.description: Base exception for all forge runtime errors.

error_types.name: ConfigurationError
error_types.description: Raised when configuration is missing, invalid, or misconfigured.

error_types.name: RuntimeNotInitializedError
error_types.description: Raised when an operation requires an initialized runtime but it is not.

error_types.name: ModuleNotFoundError
error_types.description: Raised when a requested module is not registered in the runtime.

error_types.name: ModuleRegistrationError
error_types.description: Raised when module registration fails (e.g., duplicate registration or invalid name).

error_types.name: CircularDependencyError
error_types.description: Raised when a circular dependency is detected between modules during initialization order resolution.

error_types.name: ModuleStateError
error_types.description: Raised when a module operation is invalid for its current lifecycle state.

error_types.name: EventError
error_types.description: Raised when an event bus operation fails.

error_types.name: HealthCheckError
error_types.description: Raised when a health check fails critically.

classes.name: ForgeModule
classes.description: Base class for all forge modules. Subclasses must define a 'name' property. Can optionally declare 'dependencies' list and implement setup(), teardown(), and health_check().

classes.name: ForgeRuntime
classes.description: Central runtime coordinator that manages module lifecycle (register/setup/teardown), dependency injection, event bus, and config. Call init() after registering all modules.
classes.example:
runtime = ForgeRuntime()
runtime.register(ConfigModule())
runtime.register(LogModule())
runtime.register(AIModule())
await runtime.init()
# ... use runtime ...
await runtime.teardown()

classes.name: Container
classes.description: Lightweight DI container. Modules are registered by type and resolved by type. Supports singleton scoping and dependency graph validation.

classes.name: EventBus
classes.description: In-process event emitter/subscriber for decoupled module communication. Supports wildcard patterns ('ai.*') and lifecycle hooks.

classes.name: TraceContext
classes.description: Immutable trace context holding trace_id and span_id for distributed tracing propagation.

classes.name: ModuleLifecycleState
classes.description: Enum of module lifecycle states: UNREGISTERED, REGISTERED, SETTING_UP, READY, TEARING_DOWN, STOPPED.

functions.name: in_span
functions.signature: in_span(name: str, attributes: dict[str, Any] | None = None) -> AsyncIterator[None]
functions.description: Async context manager that creates an OpenTelemetry span. No-op if OpenTelemetry is not configured.

functions.name: init_otel
functions.signature: init_otel(service_name: str, endpoint: str | None = None) -> None
functions.description: Initialize OpenTelemetry SDK. Configures trace exporter and meter provider. No-op if opentelemetry packages are not installed.

functions.name: get_trace_id
functions.description: Get the current trace_id from contextvars, or None if not in an active trace.

functions.name: set_trace_id
functions.description: Set the current trace_id in contextvars.

functions.name: get_span_id
functions.description: Get the current span_id from contextvars.

functions.name: generate_trace_id
functions.description: Generate a new random trace_id (16 hex characters).

functions.name: generate_span_id
functions.description: Generate a new random span_id (16 hex characters).

functions.name: get_baggage
functions.description: Get all baggage items from the current context.

functions.name: set_baggage
functions.description: Set a baggage item in the current context.

functions.name: get_baggage_item
functions.description: Get a specific baggage item by key.

functions.name: reset_baggage
functions.description: Clear all baggage items from the current context.

functions.name: get_meter
functions.description: Get an OpenTelemetry meter for creating instruments. Returns None if OTel is not initialized.

functions.name: get_tracer
functions.description: Get an OpenTelemetry tracer for creating spans. Returns None if OTel is not initialized.

functions.name: is_otel_available
functions.description: Returns True if OpenTelemetry packages are installed and initialized.

forge.events.description: In-process event bus for decoupled module communication. Supports typed event payloads, async handlers, lifecycle hooks, wildcard subscriptions, and event history for late-joining subscribers.

error_types.name: EventError
error_types.description: Raised when an event bus operation fails.

classes.name: EventsModule
classes.description: ForgeModule that provides the event bus. Wraps EventBus with lifecycle integration and event history buffer.

classes.name: EventBusWrapper
classes.description: Wrapper around EventBus with module-level convenience methods: emit(), on(), once().

classes.name: EventRecord
classes.signature: EventRecord(event: str, payload: dict[str, Any] | None, timestamp: float) -> EventRecord
classes.description: Record of a past event with its payload and timestamp. Used for event history replay.

classes.name: Handler
classes.description: Type alias for async event handler: Callable[[str, dict[str, Any] | None], Awaitable[None]].

forge.featureflags.description: Feature flag system with boolean, percentage rollout, and user-segment evaluation. Supports in-memory and Redis-backed storage, consistent-hash based percentage rollouts, and evaluation context.

error_types.name: FeatureFlagError
error_types.description: Base exception for feature flag errors.

error_types.name: FlagNotFoundError
error_types.description: Raised when a requested flag is not found.

error_types.name: FlagEvaluationError
error_types.description: Raised when flag evaluation fails.

error_types.name: FlagStoreError
error_types.description: Raised when the flag store operation fails.

error_types.name: InvalidFlagDefinitionError
error_types.description: Raised when a flag definition is invalid or malformed.

classes.name: FeatureFlagsModule
classes.description: ForgeModule that provides feature flag evaluation infrastructure.

classes.name: FlagDefinition
classes.signature: FlagDefinition(key: str, type: FlagType, rules: list[FlagRule], default_value: bool = False, description: str = '') -> FlagDefinition
classes.description: Definition of a feature flag, including its type (boolean, percentage, segment), rules, and default value.

classes.name: FlagEvaluator
classes.description: Evaluates feature flags against an evaluation context. Supports boolean flags, percentage rollouts (consistent hash), and user-segment rules.

classes.name: EvaluationContext
classes.signature: EvaluationContext(user_id: str | None = None, attributes: dict[str, Any] = {}) -> EvaluationContext
classes.description: Context for evaluating a feature flag. Includes user_id and optional attributes for segment matching.

classes.name: EvaluationResult
classes.signature: EvaluationResult(key: str, value: bool, reason: EvaluationReason) -> EvaluationResult
classes.description: Result of a flag evaluation, including the boolean value and the reason for the decision.

classes.name: FlagRule
classes.signature: FlagRule(type: FlagType, value: bool | float | list[SegmentRule], segments: list[SegmentRule] = []) -> FlagRule
classes.description: A single rule within a flag definition, specifying how the flag should be evaluated.

classes.name: SegmentRule
classes.signature: SegmentRule(attribute: str, operator: str, value: Any) -> SegmentRule
classes.description: A rule for matching user segments by attribute comparison (eq, neq, contains, in, etc.).

classes.name: FlagType
classes.description: Enum of flag types: BOOLEAN, PERCENTAGE, SEGMENT.

classes.name: EvaluationReason
classes.description: Enum of evaluation reasons: DEFAULT, RULE_MATCH, PERCENTAGE_ROLLOUT, SEGMENT_MATCH.

classes.name: MemoryFlagStore
classes.signature: MemoryFlagStore(flags: dict[str, FlagDefinition] | None = None) -> MemoryFlagStore
classes.description: In-memory flag store for development and testing.

classes.name: RedisFlagStore
classes.description: Redis-backed flag store for production use with multiple service instances.

forge.storage.description: File storage abstraction with local filesystem, S3, GCS, and Azure Blob adapters. Provides unified upload/download/list/delete operations.

error_types.name: StorageError
error_types.description: Base exception for storage errors.

error_types.name: StorageNotFoundError
error_types.description: Raised when a file or path is not found.

error_types.name: StorageUploadError
error_types.description: Raised when a file upload fails.

error_types.name: StorageDownloadError
error_types.description: Raised when a file download fails.

error_types.name: StorageDeleteError
error_types.description: Raised when a file delete fails.

error_types.name: StorageListError
error_types.description: Raised when listing files fails.

error_types.name: StorageConnectionError
error_types.description: Raised when connection to the storage backend fails.

error_types.name: StoragePermissionError
error_types.description: Raised when permission is denied for a storage operation.

classes.name: StorageModule
classes.description: ForgeModule that provides storage abstraction with pluggable backends.

classes.name: StorageBackend
classes.description: Abstract base class for storage backends. Defines the protocol for upload, download, list, delete, and exists operations.

classes.name: FileInfo
classes.signature: FileInfo(path: str, size: int, modified_at: datetime | None = None, content_type: str | None = None) -> FileInfo
classes.description: Metadata about a stored file, including path, size, modification time, and content type.

classes.name: LocalFilesystemAdapter
classes.description: Local filesystem storage adapter. Stores files in the specified base directory.

classes.name: S3Adapter
classes.signature: S3Adapter(bucket: str, region: str = 'us-east-1', endpoint_url: str | None = None) -> S3Adapter
classes.description: Amazon S3 storage adapter. Requires boto3 package. Supports any S3-compatible storage (MinIO, DigitalOcean Spaces, etc.).

classes.name: GCSAdapter
classes.signature: GCSAdapter(bucket: str, project: str | None = None) -> GCSAdapter
classes.description: Google Cloud Storage adapter. Requires google-cloud-storage package.

classes.name: AzureAdapter
classes.description: Azure Blob Storage adapter. Requires azure-storage-blob package.

classes.name: normalize_path
classes.description: Normalize a file path to use forward slashes and remove leading/trailing slashes.

forge.jobs.description: Background jobs and scheduling module. Provides queue-backed async job execution with automatic retry, concurrency control, dead-letter queues, cron-like scheduled tasks, and progress tracking.

error_types.name: JobError
error_types.description: Base exception for job-related errors.

error_types.name: JobNotFoundError
error_types.description: Raised when a job is not found.

error_types.name: JobExecutionError
error_types.description: Raised when a job execution fails.

classes.name: JobsModule
classes.description: ForgeModule that provides background job processing and scheduling infrastructure.

classes.name: JobDefinition
classes.signature: JobDefinition(func: Callable, queue: str = 'default', retry: int = 3, timeout: float = 300.0) -> JobDefinition
classes.description: Definition of a job type. Created by the @job decorator. Supports enqueue() for adding jobs to the queue.

classes.name: ScheduleDefinition
classes.description: Definition of a scheduled job. Created by the @schedule decorator with a cron expression.

classes.name: Job
classes.signature: Job(id: str, name: str, kwargs: dict[str, Any], status: JobStatus) -> Job
classes.description: A queued job instance with unique ID, status tracking, and execution metadata.

classes.name: JobQueue
classes.description: Queue implementation for job storage and retrieval. Supports FIFO ordering, priority, and retry with backoff.

classes.name: JobStatus
classes.description: Enum of job statuses: PENDING, RUNNING, COMPLETED, FAILED, RETRYING, CANCELLED.

classes.name: JobResult
classes.signature: JobResult(job_id: str, status: JobStatus, result: Any = None, error: str | None = None) -> JobResult
classes.description: Result of a job execution, including final status and optional error information.

classes.name: Scheduler
classes.description: Cron-based scheduler that manages scheduled job execution. Runs in a background task.

classes.name: ScheduledJob
classes.signature: ScheduledJob(name: str, cron: CronExpression, func: Callable) -> ScheduledJob
classes.description: A scheduled job combining a cron expression with an async function.

classes.name: CronExpression
classes.description: Parsed cron expression with next-run-time calculation. Supports standard 5-field cron syntax.

classes.name: QueueBackend
classes.description: Abstract base class for queue storage backends.

classes.name: MemoryBackend
classes.description: In-memory queue backend for development and testing.

classes.name: RedisBackend
classes.description: Redis-backed queue backend for production use. Supports persistence, visibility timeouts, and dead-letter queues.

functions.name: job
functions.signature: job(queue: str = 'default', retry: int = 3, timeout: float = 300.0) -> Callable
functions.description: Decorator that marks an async function as a background job. Adds an enqueue() method to the decorated function.
functions.example:
@job(queue='emails', retry=3)
async def send_email(user_id: int, template: str): ...

# Enqueue for background execution
await send_email.enqueue(user_id=42, template='welcome')

functions.name: schedule
functions.description: Decorator that schedules an async function to run on a cron schedule.

forge.crud.description: Template-based CRUD code generation for FastAPI. Generates Create/Read/Update/Delete route handlers from a Pydantic schema with pagination, filtering, sorting, and optional soft-delete support.

error_types.name: CrudError
error_types.description: Base exception for CRUD-related errors.

error_types.name: CrudGenerationError
error_types.description: Raised when CRUD code generation fails.

error_types.name: SchemaValidationError
error_types.description: Raised when the provided schema is invalid for CRUD generation.

error_types.name: TemplateNotFoundError
error_types.description: Raised when the CRUD template file is not found.

classes.name: CrudModule
classes.description: ForgeModule that provides CRUD generation capabilities.

classes.name: CrudGenerator
classes.description: Generates CRUD route handlers from a Pydantic schema configuration. Produces create, read, update, delete, and list endpoints with pagination.

classes.name: CrudGeneratorConfig
classes.signature: CrudGeneratorConfig(schema_name: str, model_name: str, table_name: str, primary_key: str = 'id', primary_key_type: str = 'int') -> CrudGeneratorConfig
classes.description: Configuration for CRUD code generation. Specifies schema names, model names, table names, and key types.

classes.name: CrudOperation
classes.description: Enum of CRUD operations: CREATE, READ, UPDATE, DELETE, LIST.

classes.name: FieldInfo
classes.signature: FieldInfo(name: str, type: str, required: bool, default: Any = None) -> FieldInfo
classes.description: Information about a single field in a CRUD schema.

functions.name: generate_crud
functions.description: Convenience function to generate and return a FastAPI APIRouter with CRUD endpoints from a config.
